notes: CARTO has no clean public machine-readable OpenAPI, so this spec was hand-authored from CARTO's SQL API and Import API developer docs. Scope: the adaptor exposes only two HTTP operations, sql() and addRow(), both of which issue a superagent GET to https://{account}.carto.com/api/v2/sql with q and api_key query params (addRow builds an INSERT via json-sql then sends it to the same endpoint). This spec models that SQL API GET plus the equivalent POST form, and additionally covers the CARTO Import API (create/list/get import jobs) which is part of the same platform surface, to provide useful seed collections. Response shapes (SqlResponse: rows/time/fields/total_rows; SqlError: error[]; async import job with item_queue_id/state) reflect documented CARTO behavior. Server uses an {account} subdomain variable defaulting to 'localhost'. Caveat: exact Import API job field set is approximated from CARTO docs and the carto-python client and may vary by CARTO version.
